channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Planned Parenthood set for massive taxpayer windfall if Senate fails to act
item.description: A coalition of the top anti-abortion groups in the country is urging Senate leadership to deliver the “most meaningful pro-taxpayer reforms Congress c...

item.title: Republicans eye ending government shutdowns forever over fears Dems will do it again
item.description: Senate Republicans explore adding shutdown prevention measures to their budget reconciliation package as the DHS closure stretches past 60 days.

item.title: Alito Knows ‘This Is A War’ For America And Wants His Clerks To Know It Too
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito is open-eyed about the ongoing “war” for the future of the United States — and he wants his law clerks to do their ...

item.title: Rep. David Scott dies suddenly just weeks before primary election in bid for 13th term
item.description: Rep. David Scott has died after representing his Georgia district for more than two decades. He was 80 years old.

item.title: How Justice Alito Assembled The Coalition That Sent Roe To The Dustbin Of History
item.description: Alito was seen as the 'best person for the job' because 'one of his greatest strengths is keeping a majority opinion together,' writes Hemingway.

item.title: Trump claims Iran 'starving for cash,' 'collapsing financially' after extending ceasefire
item.description: President Donald Trump claims Iran is collapsing financially and losing $500 million a day as he extends the ceasefire and maintains a U.S. blockade o...

item.title: Why Trump’s Historic Defense-Budget Request Matters
item.description: Now it’s time for Congress to do the difficult work.

item.title: Got drumsticks? Bill would allow SNAP participants to buy hot rotisserie chicken
item.description: The Hot Rotisserie Chicken Act aims to kill two birds with one stone: affordability and nutrition.

item.title: Rep Ilhan Omar's daughter strikes hard-core communist pose as mom battles claims she is rich
item.description: Questions surround Rep. Ilhan Omar's daughter Isra Hirsi and her ties to pro-communist organizations, including CodePink and The People's Forum.

item.title: Pulp Friction Between the Military and the Movies
item.description: How Secretary Hegseth strategized the war of cultural ownership.

item.title: Fraud Is Costing Taxpayers Up To Half A Trillion Dollars A Year, And We’re Not Doing Enough To Stop It
item.description: The Trump administration has taken a series of significant steps against fraud, but so far they're mostly going it alone.

item.title: Visa CMO: AI agents are your new customers — here’s how to sell to them
item.description: As AI takes over consumer decision-making, companies must rethink everything from product data to brand purpose — or risk being invisible.

item.title: Trump announces Iranian female protesters won't be executed
item.description: President Trump announced on social media Wednesday that the eight Iranian women scheduled to be executed by the regime now won't be.

item.title: SPLC Indicted for Gain-of-Function Research into Racism
item.description: The scurrilous left-wing group had to pay for racism to meet demand. But the criminal case may not stick.

item.title: Border agents uncover RPG launcher, cache of rifles hidden in vehicle heading to Mexico
item.description: Officers discovered an RPG launcher and over 20 weapons concealed in a Lexus during a border inspection, preventing arms from reaching Mexican cartels...

item.title: ‘FBI Does It Too’: Media Hacks Go All Out To Downplay SPLC Indictment
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche summed up the case perfectly by saying, 'The SPLC is manufacturing racism to justify its existence.'

item.title: Federal appeals court blocks California law requiring federal agents to wear badges or ID because it ‘attempts to directly regulate the U.S.’
item.description: The three-judge panel said the law violated the Constitution. California lawyers say the law applied evenly to all law enforcement officers.

item.title: Best Buy CEO Corie Barry is stepping down: How she went from architect of a comeback to cautionary tale
item.description: Shares are up only 6% since she was appointed in 2019, vastly underperforming the S&P 500.

item.title: Virginia Votes Against Its Citizens While National Republicans Sleep
item.description: Virginia barely passed a partisan redistricting that will be hard to undo.

item.title: David’s Bridal exec has a warning for every CEO obsessed with AI’s return-on-investment
item.description: David's Bridal CBO Elina Vilk says companies chasing a discrete AI return-on-investment are asking the wrong question—and missing the real opportunity...

item.title: Supreme Court ponders deporting green card holders
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Wednesday to make it easier to deport green card holders with criminal records who leave the count...

item.title: Appeals court blocks California's No Vigilantes law forcing ICE to display names while on duty
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled against California Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday, putting on hold his state law that tried to force federal immigration...

item.title: The Fight to Expose Left-Wing Violence
item.description: Starting today, NR donors who give $250 and up will receive a free, signed copy of my forthcoming book on the subject.

item.title: Cursor’s 25-year-old CEO is a former Google intern who just inked a $60 billion deal with SpaceX
item.description: From Google intern at 18 to a billionaire at 25, Cursor CEO Michael Truell's rise is one of Silicon Valley's fastest.

item.title: New Records Prove Biden FBI Spying On Congress ‘Wasn’t An Isolated Incident’
item.description: If the Biden administration was defined by one thing, it was its consistent and careful targeting of Democrats’ political enemies.
